title: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

seo.title: Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make delicious Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) at home! Our easy, authentic recipe brings the taste of Korea to your dinner table.

easy Korean BBQ Short Ribs recipe

h2Headings: What is Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs)?

seo.imageAlt: Platter of grilled Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi) garnished with sesame seeds and scallions
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, savory, and slightly sweet flavors of homemade Korean BBQ Short Ribs (Galbi).

description: Galbi, a quintessential dish of Korean BBQ, features tender beef short ribs marinated in a flavorful blend of soy sauce, garlic, and fruit. The ribs are then grilled to perfection, resulting in a succulent, savory, and slightly sweet experience. This recipe brings the authentic taste of a Korean BBQ restaurant right to your home kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Rinse the flanken-cut short ribs under cold water to remove any bone fragments. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. You can score the meat lightly across the grain to help with tenderness and marinade absorption, if desired.

instructions.title: Make the Galbi Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the soy sauce, water, brown sugar, grated Asian pear, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, mirin, and black pepper. Whisk well until the sugar is fully dissolved and all ingredients are evenly mixed.

instructions.title: Marinate the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Add the prepared short ribs to the marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor. Turn the ribs occasionally to ensure even marination.

instructions.title: Prepare for Grilling
instructions.text: Remove the short ribs from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes before grilling to bring them closer to room temperature. Preheat your grill (charcoal or gas) to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Galbi
instructions.text: Place the marinated short ribs on the hot grill. Grill for 3-5 minutes per side, depending on thickness and desired doneness. The ribs should have beautiful char marks and be cooked through but still tender. Avoid overcooking to prevent dryness.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Galbi from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before slicing or serving whole. Garnish generously with toasted sesame seeds and sliced green onions. Serve immediately with rice and various banchan (Korean side dishes).

tips: To achieve truly tender Galbi, ensure you use flanken-cut short ribs and marinate them for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight, allowing the enzymes from the Asian pear to tenderize the meat.
For an extra layer of flavor and slight sweetness, you can substitute a small amount of the brown sugar with honey or maple syrup in the marinade.
Galbi is best served hot off the grill. Pair it with fresh lettuce wraps, kimchi, steamed rice, and other Korean side dishes (banchan) for a complete and authentic Korean BBQ experience.
article:
Galbi, also known as Korean BBQ Short Ribs, is a quintessential dish in Korean cuisine, celebrated worldwide for its incredibly tender meat and addictively sweet and savory marinade. Often the star of any Korean dinner table, this dish transforms humble beef short ribs into an unforgettable culinary experience. Whether you’re a seasoned home cook or just beginning your journey into Korean dinner recipes, mastering Galbi is a rewarding endeavor that promises to impress. This article will guide you through everything you need to know to create the perfect Galbi in your own kitchen.

## What is Galbi (Korean BBQ Short Ribs)?
Galbi, meaning "rib" in Korean, typically refers to a dish made with beef short ribs, though pork ribs are also used for a similar preparation called Dwaeji Galbi. The most common cut for beef Galbi in Western markets is the "flanken-cut" or "LA Galbi," where the ribs are cut thin across the bone, resulting in flat, rectangular pieces containing several small bone segments. This specific cut allows for quicker marinating and cooking, as well as easier consumption. The magic of Galbi lies in its signature marinade – a rich blend of soy sauce, sugar, garlic, sesame oil, and often fruit (like Asian pear or kiwi) which not only imparts a distinctive flavor but also acts as a natural tenderizer, ensuring every bite of these Korean BBQ Short Ribs is melt-in-your-mouth delicious.

## Ingredients for Korean BBQ Short Ribs
Crafting the perfect Galbi starts with selecting quality ingredients and assembling a balanced marinade.

**For the Ribs:**
*   2-3 lbs beef short ribs (flanken-cut/LA Galbi)

**For the Marinade:**
*   1/2 cup soy sauce (low sodium preferred)
*   1/4 cup brown sugar (or a mix of sugar and honey)
*   1/4 cup mirin (rice wine)
*   1/4 cup Asian pear juice (or grated Asian pear/apple, or even kiwi for tenderizing)
*   1/4 cup water or beef broth
*   4-6 cloves garlic, minced
*   1-inch piece ginger, grated
*   2 green onions, chopped (white and green parts, separated)
*   1 tbsp sesame oil
*   1/2 tsp black pepper
*   Optional: 1 tbsp gochujang (Korean chili paste) for a spicy kick

**For Garnish:**
*   Toasted sesame seeds
*   Extra chopped green onions

## How to Make Galbi: Step-by-Step Guide

**Step 1: Prepare the Short Ribs**
Begin by thoroughly rinsing the short ribs under cold running water to remove any bone fragments or excess blood. For best results, you can soak the ribs in cold water for 30 minutes to an hour, changing the water once or twice, to draw out more impurities. Once rinsed, pat the ribs completely dry with paper towels. This step is crucial for achieving a good sear later. If your ribs are not flanken-cut, you may need to butterfly them or score them deeply to allow the marinade to penetrate.

**Step 2: Prepare the Marinade**
In a large bowl, whisk together all the marinade ingredients: soy sauce, brown sugar, mirin, Asian pear juice (or grated pear/apple/kiwi), water, minced garlic, grated ginger, the white parts of the chopped green onions, sesame oil, and black pepper. If you desire spicy Korean BBQ Short Ribs, add the optional gochujang here. Mix until the sugar is fully dissolved.

**Step 3: Marinate the Ribs**
Place the dried short ribs into a large, non-reactive bowl, a resealable plastic bag, or a shallow dish. Pour the prepared marinade over the ribs, ensuring all pieces are thoroughly coated. If using a bag, press out as much air as possible before sealing. Refrigerate for at least 6 hours, but ideally overnight, or even up to 24 hours. The longer the marinating time, the more flavorful and tender your Galbi will be.

**Step 4: Grill the Galbi**
When ready to cook, remove the ribs from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes beforehand to bring them closer to room temperature. Preheat your grill (charcoal, gas, or even a cast-iron griddle pan) to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking. Remove the ribs from the marinade, letting any excess drip off (do not wipe it off).

Place the ribs on the hot grill, making sure not to overcrowd the grill, which can steam the meat instead of searing it. Cook for 3-5 minutes per side, depending on the thickness of the ribs and your desired doneness. Turn frequently to ensure even cooking and to prevent burning due to the sugar in the marinade. Look for beautiful caramelization and a slight char. The internal temperature should reach 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare.

**Step 5: Rest and Serve**
Once cooked, transfer the Galbi to a cutting board and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in more tender and flavorful meat. After resting, use kitchen shears or a knife to cut the meat off the bones into bite-sized pieces.

## Tips for Perfect Korean BBQ Short Ribs
*   **Quality Meat Matters:** Opt for well-marbled beef short ribs for the most tender and flavorful result.
*   **Don't Skimp on Marinating Time:** While 6 hours is acceptable, overnight (12-24 hours) truly allows the flavors to meld and the tenderizers to work their magic on your Korean BBQ Short Ribs.
*   **The Power of Fruit:** Asian pear, kiwi, or even grated apple are not just for sweetness; their enzymes are excellent natural tenderizers. If using kiwi, be mindful not to marinate for too long (over 6-8 hours), as it can break down the meat too much.
*   **Achieve the Perfect Char:** High heat is essential for developing that beautiful caramelized crust. Don't be afraid of a little char, as it adds depth of flavor.
*   **Clean Grill Grates:** Ensure your grill grates are clean and well-oiled to prevent the ribs from sticking.
*   **Charcoal for Authenticity:** While gas grills work perfectly fine, a charcoal grill imparts a smoky flavor that is characteristic of traditional Korean BBQ.

## Serving Suggestions for Galbi
Galbi is a celebratory dish, and how you serve it can elevate the entire dining experience.
*   **Ssam (Wraps):** The most traditional way to enjoy Galbi is to wrap it in fresh lettuce leaves (like red leaf, green leaf, or butter lettuce), perilla leaves (kkaennip), or even thinly sliced pickled radish. Add a dollop of ssamjang (a spicy, savory dipping sauce made from gochujang and doenjang), a slice of raw garlic, or a piece of green chili.
*   **Banchan (Side Dishes):** No Korean meal is complete without a variety of banchan. Kimchi (fermented cabbage), seasoned spinach (sigeumchi namul), seasoned bean sprouts (kongnamul muchim), and pickled radishes are excellent accompaniments to these Korean dinner recipes.
*   **Steamed Rice:** A warm bowl of fluffy white rice is essential for balancing the rich flavors of the Galbi.
*   **Soup:** A light soup like Doenjang Jjigae (fermented soybean paste stew) can complement the meal beautifully.
*   **Beverages:** Soju, Korean beer, or refreshing barley tea are popular drink pairings.

## Variations & Substitutions for Korean Short Ribs
*   **Spicy Galbi:** As mentioned, adding gochujang (Korean chili paste) to the marinade will introduce a lovely heat to your Korean Short Ribs. Adjust the amount to your spice preference.
*   **Boneless Galbi:** If you prefer to avoid bones, boneless beef short ribs can be used. Adjust cooking time accordingly as they will cook faster.
*   **Different Cuts:** While flanken-cut is popular, traditional bone-in short ribs (often butterflied to resemble a large, flat steak) can also be used. This requires longer marinating and cooking times.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan Adaptation:** While not true Galbi, you can adapt the marinade for thick slices of firm tofu, portobello mushrooms, or seitan for a delicious plant-based alternative.
*   **Sweetness Levels:** Adjust the amount of brown sugar or honey in the marinade to suit your taste. Some prefer a sweeter profile, while others like it more savory.

Galbi is more than just a meal; it's an experience. The irresistible aroma of the sizzling meat, the explosion of sweet and savory flavors, and the communal joy of sharing a delicious Korean BBQ spread make it a truly special dish. With this guide, you now have all the tools to create fantastic Korean BBQ Short Ribs right in your own home. So gather your ingredients, fire up the grill, and get ready to enjoy one of the most beloved Korean dinner recipes.
